title: gentiana triflora subsp. japonica
intro: Gentiana triflora subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family that is native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Gentiana triflora subsp. japonica include its potential to treat various conditions such as colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to support digestive health and relieve digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Gentiana triflora subsp. japonica is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are used to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
